title: Dataflow State
use_for: defining source state, derived state, and intermediate pipeline products
deciding what should be retained between runs
keeping data ownership explicit across pipeline stages
rules: separate source truth from derived products
retain derived products only when reuse is cheaper than recompute
key retained products by stable identity
make ownership of intermediate state explicit
procedure: List derived products produced by each stage.
Mark which products are retained and why.

primary_when: the request is specifically about retained pipeline products, derived state, or source-versus-derived ownership
must_not_own_when: the request is mainly about stage order or dispatch model

solves: what data belongs to source truth versus derived pipeline outputs
how to retain intermediate products without losing ownership clarity

core_principles: Derived products should exist to reduce repeated work, not to blur ownership.
Intermediate data must be owned by a stage or clearly shared by policy.
constraints: Derived state must not silently become authoritative input.
Retained products must have explicit invalidation triggers.
decision_rules: Retain a product when many downstream reads are cheaper than rerunning its stage repeatedly.
Discard a product when ownership and invalidation cost exceed its reuse benefit.

preferred_patterns: retained stage outputs keyed by identity
explicit invalidation rules per retained product
avoid_patterns: derived state masquerading as source truth
shared mutable intermediate products with unclear owners

recipes.name: Retain reusable stage outputs safely
recipes.when: Use when downstream stages repeatedly consume the same derived product.
steps: Name the source input and derived product separately.
Retain the derived product behind an identity and invalidation rule.
Keep downstream consumers dependent on the derived product contract, not on source mutation details.

pitfalls: Treating cached derived data as if it were source truth.
Sharing mutable intermediate state across unrelated stages.
review_checklist: Is source truth separate from derived state?
Do retained products have explicit ownership?
Can consumers tell what product they depend on?

references.title: Local Reference: Dataflow State
references.interpretation: Use this first for source-versus-derived ownership and retained intermediate product rules.
references.why: Use as the primary local guide for state and retained pipeline products.
